recommended_actions.title: Follow SOP: DRV-410 Drive Current Above Normal Range
recommended_actions.detail: Per SOP section 'DRV-410 Drive Current Above Normal Range': Check roller surface condition for contamination, wear, or material buildup.; Inspect drive belt tension and alignment.; Check bearing condition and lubrication.

recommended_actions.title: Follow SOP: TEN-204 Web Tension Oscillation
recommended_actions.detail: Per SOP section 'TEN-204 Web Tension Oscillation': Check dancer roller position feedback.; Check tension controller response after recipe switch.; Inspect drive current trend and roller surface contamination.

recommended_actions.title: Follow SOP: QCS-318 Thickness Drift
recommended_actions.detail: Per SOP section 'QCS-318 Thickness Drift': Correlate drift time with temperature, tension and line speed changes.; Isolate affected material roll range.; Request quality engineer review before release.

recommended_actions.title: Follow SOP: Roller Contamination Response
recommended_actions.detail: Per SOP section 'Roller Contamination Response': Prioritize roller surface inspection – contamination can cause both elevated drive current and tension instability.; Check if recent coating material change or process adjustment increased residue buildup.; Schedule roller cleaning during next planned stop if immediate stop is not warranted.

recommended_actions.title: Quality engineer review of affected material
recommended_actions.detail: Isolate affected material roll range. Request quality engineer review before release. Document thickness deviation for batch record.

recommended_actions.title: Notify production supervisor
recommended_actions.detail: High-priority root causes detected: Drive current resistance from roller surface contamination, Web tension closed-loop response lag, Coating thickness drift and yield risk. Production supervisor should be notified for resource allocation and scheduling decisions.

recommended_actions.title: Confirm safety procedures before equipment intervention
recommended_actions.detail: All equipment inspections and interventions must follow factory safety procedures. Do not bypass interlocks or overcurrent protection. Final equipment operation requires on-site engineer confirmation.
